instruction: Lay out the top-level part_tree with one node per required_subsystem, a global datum scheme, the overall envelope, and stub interfaces between subsystems. No detailed geometry.

disciplines.structures: Load-bearing geometry, frames, spars, skins, joints, stiffness
disciplines.mechanisms: Linkages, hinges, gears, cams, deployment, tilt/rotation devices
disciplines.actuation: Motors, servos, brakes, reducers and their mounting/replacement
disciplines.power: Battery, solar generation, charging, distribution, connectors
disciplines.comms: Antennas, radios, data links and their placement/clearance
disciplines.payload: Mission equipment bay, sensor mounts, removable inserts
disciplines.integration: Datums, interfaces, harness routing, service access, assembly order

required_subsystems.function: Wing planform, airfoil section evidence, winglets, elevon/control surface cutouts with hinges
Main spar, ribs, carry-through, fuselage frames; continuous load path
Motor pod / pusher prop mount reference article with bolt pattern and cooling path (non-operational)
Slide-in li-ion battery pack bay on CG rail, BMS and connector access
GPS, pitot boom, receiver/telemetry boards, antenna routing away from power leads
Non-weapon camera/gimbal sensor pod bay with removable insert and window
Servo pockets, control horns, pushrod/linkage channels
Wing/fuselage datums, harness raceways, hatch access, connection types and load-path closure

completeness_rule: Every required_subsystem must appear in part_tree with at least one evidence_feature in geometry_ops; a missing subsystem is a scorecard failure, not a style issue.

criteria.loop_feedback: Map every P0 feature target into a named part_tree child and at least one geometry_op id or target.
Add coordinate-bearing geometry_ops across upstream/downstream/side/service regions instead of stacking features.
Replace box-only geometry with interface bosses, service pockets, drilled holes, channels, ribs, and labels.
Name the mechanism type behind every motion claim and add its pivot holes, links, or cam/gear reference article geometry.
Add visible datum pads or axis/rail/socket features and label their mating function.
Add clearance_mm or tolerance notes for hinge pins, slide rails, sockets, and removable cartridges.
Add at least three adjacent interfaces: mount, service, electrical/data, flow, or inspection datum.
Classify each mating diameter as clearance/transition/press fit and add a numeric allowance instead of line-to-line nominals.
Snap hole, pin, and bearing dimensions to standard catalog sizes and name the intended hardware in callouts.
For each interface pick a connection type from the taxonomy, state its DOF (0/1R/1T/1R1T/3R), and add a numeric clearance and the matching geometry feature.
State an installation order and verify each joint is reachable when it is made; reorder or add access openings where a part would trap another.
Name one primary datum part, route each part's location back to it through one parent datum, and keep critical fits off the primary datum to limit stack-up.
Trace each part's main load to the base through named interfaces; upgrade any load-bearing joint from snap/adhesive to bolted/pinned/clamped and add the fitting where the path breaks.
Drive the next version toward fewer justified interfaces, consolidated printable parts that keep service access, and closed load paths; add redundancy only at safety-critical joints.
Merge interfaces that exist for no service/motion/manufacture/load reason; keep a split only when it earns its joint.

connection_taxonomy.rule: Name each inter-part interface with one of these connection types + its DOF + a numeric clearance, instead of a vague 'mount'.

operability_profile.operating_principle: Fixed-wing UAV architecture that carries lift through spar/rib stations, routes propulsion/power through the fuselage, and isolates payload/sensor bays.
